description: This v-neck, long-sleeved, abaca female upper garment is embroidered on the hem, shoulders, cuffs, and all edges. The bodice is dominantly dyed red, while both sleeves are dyed blue-black, similar to the Blaan saol or saul upper garment. The neckline and hem are piped with a blue strip of cotton, while the cuffs are piped with a red strip of cotton, all sewn with abaca threads in a zigzag pattern. The embroidery is of geometric designs: double X's, triangles, horizontal upright zigzag patterns, and fern-like patterns on the shoulders and cuffs.
condition: Fair and stable condition. Small discoloration on the right chest; fading on the upper neck area; loose at the armpits; a few creases and folds.

images.alt: Red-bodiced abaca upper garment with black dyed sleeves banded in gold and red geometric embroidery, front view with V-neck
Reverse of the garment, its red back panel and black sleeves trimmed with the same embroidered zigzag bands

rights: Images courtesy of the Ethnologisches Museum, Staatliche Museen zu Berlin.
